### Stock Market Today: Nasdaq Edges Higher After Tech Earnings

*First-quarter GDP misses expectations; Iran concerns keep oil prices elevated*

- **URL:** https://www.wsj.com/livecoverage/stock-market-today-dow-sp-500-nasdaq-04-30-2026?mod=rss_markets_main
- **Published:** 2026-04-22 08:21 UTC
- **Section:** Markets

**Context:** Live US equities tape with two stagflationary signals: Q1 GDP undershoot and oil bid up by Iran-war risk premium. The combination favors energy and short-duration over the broad index — XLE / VDE for energy beta, US 2Y TIPS or short-duration Treasuries via VTIP / IBTA.L (UCITS) for the inflation-protection leg, and a fade in long-duration tech if the GDP miss persists into Q2 prints.

### Activist Hedge Fund Makes Nearly $3 Billion Offer to Buy Meineke Owner

*ADW Capital says private-equity firm Roark Capital is mismanaging Driven and offers a roughly 40% premium to buy the company.*

- **URL:** https://www.wsj.com/business/deals/activist-hedge-fund-makes-nearly-3-billion-offer-to-buy-meineke-owner-b3b265d5?mod=rss_markets_main
- **Published:** 2026-04-22 12:00 UTC · AnnaMaria Andriotis
- **Section:** Markets

**Context:** ADW Capital pitching a ~40% premium counter to Roark's existing private-equity ownership of Driven Brands (Meineke parent). Driven is currently private after Roark's take-private, so the trade isn't directly listable, but the auto-aftermarket comp set is — AAP, ORLY, AZO, MNRO. Watch for read-throughs to whether sponsor-owned aftermarket platforms get re-marked higher across the sector.

- **URL:** https://www.wsj.com/finance/stocks/its-a-blowout-earnings-season-heres-why-that-might-be-a-bad-sign-465237cd?mod=rss_markets_main
- **Published:** 2026-04-22 10:24 UTC · Spencer Jakab
- **Section:** Markets

**Context:** Jakab's Streetwise column flags the historical pattern that unusually strong earnings seasons mean-revert; Meta's reaction is the canonical example of a beat that still got punished. For an SPY/IVV holder, this argues for a quality / low-vol tilt (QUAL, USMV) over equal-weight beta (RSP) into the back half of the season rather than chasing the print.

### The Clock Is Ticking for Big Tech to Make AI Pay

*Depreciation charges are eating into earnings at Microsoft, Alphabet, Meta and Amazon.*

- **URL:** https://www.wsj.com/tech/ai/the-clock-is-ticking-for-big-tech-to-make-ai-pay-b5048a8e?mod=rss_markets_main
- **Published:** 2026-04-22 09:30 UTC · Asa Fitch
- **Section:** Markets

**Context:** AI capex is rolling into the P&L as depreciation at MSFT, GOOGL, META, AMZN, compressing operating margins even as revenue grows. The cleanest pair is long the picks-and-shovels beneficiary (NVDA, AVGO, networking like ANET) versus the hyperscaler with the most stretched capex/FCF gap — META and GOOGL screen worst on that metric right now.

### A Wild Short Squeeze on Avis&#x2019;s Stock Takes Another Surprising Turn

*The rental car company says a hedge-fund firm behind the big swings owes it money because of a niche trading rule.*

- **URL:** https://www.wsj.com/finance/stocks/a-wild-short-squeeze-on-aviss-stock-takes-another-surprising-turn-854d6f65?mod=rss_markets_main
- **Published:** 2026-04-21 22:29 UTC · Ben Dummett
- **Section:** Markets

**Context:** Niche reg-SHO mechanic means a hedge fund ends up owing CAR money post-squeeze. Avis is structurally a fleet residual-value bet, not a clean GDP play — Hertz (HTZ) is the direct comp and remains a reorg story. Treat both as event-driven rather than core; size accordingly.

### Iran War Boosts a Fast-Growing Chinese Industry: Turning Coal Into Stuff

*Plus, rising home-insurance costs, and will stable weather become a luxury good?*

- **URL:** https://www.wsj.com/business/energy-oil/iran-war-boosts-a-fast-growing-chinese-industry-turning-coal-into-stuff-e817fd7b?mod=pls_whats_news_us_business_f
- **Published:** 2026-04-22 13:30 UTC · Ed Ballard
- **Section:** US Business

**Context:** Iran war is accelerating China's coal-to-olefins / coal-to-chemicals buildout, which is Beijing's hedge against blocked Hormuz oil. Read-through: pressure on Western petrochemical margins (DOW, LYB, BASF.DE) and a tailwind for Chinese chemical names (Sinopec 386.HK). Reinsurance angle on the home-insurance teaser: MUV2.DE, SREN.SW, RNR.

- **Section:** US Business

**Context:** Sports-streaming consolidation: private DAZN buys streaming-tech vendor ViewLift to deepen its US push. Not directly tradable, but signals continued capital flowing into OTT sports. ### Cigna Revenue Rises on Evernorth Growth

*Cigna Group logged higher profit and revenue in its latest quarter as its pharmacy-benefit business Evernorth continued to grow.*

- **URL:** https://www.wsj.com/business/earnings/cigna-revenue-rises-on-evernorth-growth-0d9eddb8?mod=pls_whats_news_us_business_f
- **Published:** 2026-04-22 10:06 UTC · Kelly Cloonan
- **Section:** US Business

**Context:** Evernorth (PBM + specialty pharmacy) is now the earnings engine for CI, mirroring the pattern at CVS / Caremark and UNH / OptumRx. PBM regulatory risk is the overhang — any vertical-integration crackdown hits all three together. Within the trio, CI screens cheapest on forward P/E; UNH has the cleanest balance sheet.

### Airbnb Hosts Prepped Their Homes for a World Cup Windfall. They&#x2019;re Still Waiting.

*Some spent years and thousands of dollars preparing their houses for the tournament. &#x201c;I don&#x2019;t think it&#x2019;s going to be the huge windfall that I once thought.&#x201d;*

- **URL:** https://www.wsj.com/business/hospitality/airbnb-hosts-prepped-their-homes-for-a-world-cup-windfall-theyre-still-waiting-fcd38b57?mod=pls_whats_news_us_business_f
- **Published:** 2026-04-22 09:30 UTC · Owen Tucker-Smith
- **Section:** US Business

**Context:** ABNB hosts who pre-invested for the 2026 World Cup are reporting weaker-than-hoped bookings — a soft sentiment data point on both Airbnb's brand and US discretionary travel demand heading into summer. The thesis read for an ETF + single-stock investor: marginally negative for ABNB (consumer-travel multiple has been demanding), neutral-to-positive for the asset-light hotel comps BKNG and MAR, which gain wallet share when host economics disappoint. Travel ETFs (JETS, AWAY) are mostly airlines and won't react. Watch ABNB's Q2 forward guidance for whether this is a teaser anecdote or a real demand miss.

## World

### Jihadists Are on the Brink of an African Caliphate

*Islamists in Mali have routed Kremlin-backed forces after confounding efforts by the West to uproot al Qaeda and its offshoots in the Sahel.*

- **URL:** https://www.wsj.com/world/africa/jihadists-are-on-the-brink-of-an-african-caliphate-831569e7
- **Published:** 2026-04-22 14:55 UTC · Benoit Faucon
- **Section:** World

**Context:** Jihadist advance in the Sahel — having now routed Russia's Wagner-successor forces — opens a gap that neither the West nor Russia is filling. Direct portfolio implications run through commodities sourced from the region: uranium (Niger is a major producer; CCJ, YCA.L UCITS, U.UN), gold (regional instability has historically supported the gold complex; GDX, GLD or UCITS PHGP.L), and French oil/EU defense exposure (TTE.PA, RHM.DE, AIR.PA). Etoro / FlatEx / Saxo all carry CCJ and the European defense names; for Danish lagerbeskatning purposes the broad commodity ETFs are taxed annually whether or not sold, which can change after-tax IRR materially. The contrarian read: this is a slow-moving thesis, not a fast-money trade — sized small as a tail-risk hedge.
